persona.name: The Quiet Mainstream Spender
persona.description: This is the largest group at 15.5% of all customers. Their transaction data came through blank, so we can't pin down exact habits. Based on cluster size alone, they likely represent a broad middle-ground of everyday banking activity.
traits: Largest single cluster in the dataset
Likely reflects average or near-average spending patterns
No extreme highs or lows in any dimension
Possibly a catch-all for customers who don't fit niche patterns
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Rare but Distinct Transactor
persona.tagline: Small group, stands out for some reason
persona.description: Only 14 customers land here — just 1.4% of all customers. That tiny size suggests something specific sets them apart, but without actual spend figures we can only guess. They may be low-frequency, high-value buyers or a very niche behavioural type.
traits: One of the smallest clusters in the entire set
Tight grouping suggests a very specific shared behaviour
Possibly infrequent but notable transaction patterns
Could represent a premium or outlier customer type
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Occasional Mid-Tier Shopper
persona.tagline: Buys sometimes, not often, not rarely
persona.description: At 4.2% of customers, this is a modest-sized group. Their placement in a 14-cluster solution hints at a recognisable pattern distinct from the larger mainstream groups. Without spend data, we suspect moderate frequency with unremarkable transaction sizes.
traits: Mid-sized cluster suggesting moderate behavioural consistency
Likely sits between casual and active spenders
Does not appear to cluster with the largest groups
Possibly irregular purchase timing
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Highly Specific Micro-Segment
persona.tagline: Tiny group, very tightly defined behaviour
persona.description: Just 7 customers — 0.7% of the base. A cluster this small in a k=14 solution almost always means the algorithm found something genuinely unusual. These customers likely share a very specific and uncommon spending pattern that sets them apart from everyone else.
traits: Second smallest cluster in the dataset
Extreme tightness implies a very narrow behavioural profile
Could represent a special customer type like a business account or bulk buyer
Unlikely to overlap with any mainstream segment
Very low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Consistent Everyday Transactor
persona.tagline: Steady and predictable, week in, week out
persona.description: Nearly 10% of all customers belong here, making this a sizeable and likely well-defined group. The cluster's distinct placement away from the two largest groups (Clusters 0 and 9) suggests a recognisable rhythm of regular spending. They are probably reliable, habitual buyers.
traits: Nearly one in ten customers fits this profile
Likely characterised by steady transaction frequency
Probably shows consistent month-over-month behaviour
Distinguished from the largest clusters by some measurable habit
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Active But Unremarkable Regular
persona.description: At 8.4% this is a sizeable cluster, likely representing a group of fairly active spenders who don't push into extreme territory on any single dimension. They are probably frequent enough to stand apart from casual users but not distinctive enough to land in a niche cluster.
traits: Large enough to represent a broad customer archetype
Likely moderate-to-high transaction frequency
Spending probably spans several common categories
No single dominant behaviour strong enough to single out
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Selective Periodic Buyer
persona.tagline: Picks their moments, doesn't spend randomly
persona.description: This group of 28 customers (2.8%) is small enough to suggest a fairly specific shared trait. Their separation into their own cluster implies they buy in a pattern — perhaps concentrated into certain times of the month or around particular categories — rather than spreading spending evenly.
traits: Small cluster suggesting a focused behavioural profile
Likely shows uneven timing between purchases
May concentrate spending around specific triggers or dates
Distinct from the larger everyday-spending clusters
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Moderate Niche Loyalist
persona.tagline: Not huge in numbers, but sticks to their habits
persona.description: Thirty-five customers (3.6%) share this profile. A cluster of this size in a 14-way split usually means a clear, repeatable pattern. The 'loyalist' framing comes from the expectation that loyalty months would be a key differentiator here, though we can't confirm without the data.
traits: Small-to-mid group suggesting a well-defined habit
Possibly longer-than-average loyalty tenure
Likely spends in a narrow set of preferred categories
Behaviour is consistent enough to form its own cluster
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Extreme Outlier
persona.tagline: Five people who behave like no one else
persona.description: Only 5 customers — the smallest cluster of all at 0.5%. In a k=14 clustering, a group this tiny almost always signals unusual or extreme values on at least one dimension. These customers likely have very high or very low figures on spend, transaction count, or loyalty that no other cluster captures.
traits: Smallest cluster in the entire dataset
Almost certainly driven by one or two extreme data points
May represent outlier accounts worth individual review
No meaningful archetype can be assigned without actual data
Lowest possible confidence due to tiny size and missing data

persona.name: The High-Volume Everyday Mainstream Customer
persona.tagline: The most common type of customer in this bank
persona.description: At 20.1% this is the single largest cluster — one in five customers. In a 14-cluster model this dominance suggests a very central, average-leaning profile. These are likely the archetypal bank customers: transacting regularly, spending across common categories, with nothing extreme pulling them into a niche group.
traits: Largest cluster — the most 'typical' customer in the dataset
Likely spans a wide range of everyday spending categories
Probably shows moderate loyalty and moderate frequency
Close to the overall population average on most metrics
Low confidence due to missing numeric data despite large size

persona.name: The Above-Average Frequency Spender
persona.tagline: Shops more often than most, keeps it normal
persona.description: At 11.3% this is the third-largest cluster. Its separation from Cluster 9 (the biggest mainstream group) suggests a measurable step up in at least one dimension — likely transaction frequency or total spend. These customers probably visit merchants more often than average without crossing into extreme territory.
traits: Third largest cluster, suggesting a broadly common profile
Likely higher transaction frequency than the overall average
Probably spread across multiple spending categories
Distinguished from Cluster 9 by one or two elevated metrics
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Infrequent High-Commitment Customer
persona.tagline: Doesn't transact much, but stays loyal
persona.description: Twenty-three customers (2.3%) make up this small cluster. The small size and distinct placement suggest a specific combination of behaviours — possibly low transaction counts paired with long loyalty tenure, meaning they stick around but don't buy often.
traits: Small cluster pointing to a specific behavioural niche
Possibly long loyalty duration with low transaction volume
May transact less frequently but with higher per-transaction value
Likely retains account for reasons beyond frequent spending
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Second-Tier Mainstream Spender
persona.tagline: Very common, very ordinary, slightly different from the top group
persona.description: This is the second-largest cluster at 14.8%, just behind Cluster 9. The two likely share many traits but are split by one distinguishing dimension — perhaps loyalty length, average transaction size, or spending frequency. Together, Clusters 9 and 12 probably cover the broad middle of the customer base.
traits: Second largest cluster, very close in size to the biggest group
Likely represents a mainstream spending profile with a subtle twist
Possibly slightly lower frequency or spend than Cluster 9
Broadly average on most behavioural metrics
Low confidence due to missing numeric data

persona.name: The Moderate Niche Periodic Transactor
persona.tagline: Falls between the mainstream and the true niche groups
persona.description: At 4.6%, this cluster sits at the border between the large mainstream groups and the smaller specialist clusters. They likely have a recognisable pattern — possibly tied to periodic large purchases or a specific spending category — that keeps them out of the bigger mainstream buckets.
traits: Mid-small cluster suggesting a moderately specific behaviour
Likely distinguished by a particular spending rhythm or category focus
Not extreme enough to land in the tiny outlier clusters
Not generic enough to merge with the large mainstream groups
Low confidence due to missing numeric data
